enriched_points.expanded_content: Tunnel Boring Machines (TBMs) exhibit high success rates, especially in favorable ground conditions, significantly enhancing project efficiency and reducing excavation time.
Cut-and-cover tunneling faces challenges like ground stability, environmental impact, and urban disruption, often leading to increased costs and project delays.
The New Austrian Tunneling Method (NATM) demonstrates adaptability in mixed ground conditions, balancing excavation efficiency with structural integrity through continuous monitoring.
Conducting a thorough cost-benefit analysis reveals that while initial investments in TBMs may be high, long-term savings from reduced labor and time are substantial.
Case studies highlight critical lessons in TBM selection, advance planning, and risk management, ultimately guiding future projects toward improved success rates.
